meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve can't collaborate (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't collaborate. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve Can't Collaborate
content.seo_description: Master user onboarding to solve collaboration bottlenecks. An advanced guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and scale.
content.answer_block: Collaboration failures stem from poor visibility. By implementing automated user onboarding, you synchronize client expectations with technical reality. Use Antigravity to scaffold the workflow and Claude Code to automate the state management, ensuring clients are guided through your SaaS architecture seamlessly, eliminating friction and redundant communication cycles.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is pinging you on Slack, and your project is stalling because they simply don't understand how to interface with the system you've built. You're trapped in a loop of 'how do I access this?' and 'why is this failing?'—a classic collaboration breakdown that kills your hourly rate and your reputation.

It is time to stop being a support desk and start being an architect. By baking collaboration directly into your user onboarding, you transform passive users into empowered stakeholders. We’re going to leverage the raw power of Antigravity and Claude Code to build a self-service onboarding flow that handles the heavy lifting, so you can focus on shipping features, not explaining them.

steps.title: Define the Collaborative State Machine
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the user states that require client intervention. We use Antigravity to define these as atomic, transition-based states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript state machine for a SaaS onboarding flow. Include states for 'Initial Setup', 'Invite Team Members', 'Configure API Keys', and 'Live Sync'. Ensure each state has a corresponding validation schema for client inputs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the state machine from Antigravity in src/lib/onboarding.ts'

steps.title: Automate User Context Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to inject context-aware tooltips and walkthroughs based on the user's progress in the onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React hook 'useOnboardingProgress' that tracks user interactions and triggers specific UI feedback components based on the state machine defined in step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --execute 'npm install @tanstack/react-query && claude add-component onboarding-walkthrough'

steps.title: Implement Collaborative Invite Gates
steps.description: Prevent collaboration deadlocks by forcing invite flows during the onboarding phase, ensuring the client adds their team early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks if a user has invited at least one team member. If not, redirect them to the /invite-team onboarding step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/middleware.ts --apply 'Add invite-gate middleware to protect dashboard routes'

steps.title: Sync State with Backend Observability
steps.description: Clients feel confident when they see progress. We link our onboarding state to a real-time progress bar.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a backend controller that emits a 'progress_updated' event to a Supabase real-time channel whenever a user completes an onboarding milestone.

steps.title: Final Verification and Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to run automated end-to-end tests on the onboarding flow to ensure no friction points remain.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Playwright test suite that simulates a new user going through the onboarding flow, inviting a team member, and reaching the dashboard.

pro_tips: Always gate the 'Invite Team' step behind a low-friction prompt; clients who invite their team early are 3x more likely to retain.
Use Antigravity to generate 'read-only' views for clients so they can see the progress without accidentally breaking your production configuration.
Inject a 'Help' button that contextually detects the current onboarding state and pulls documentation from your project's README.
Never hardcode onboarding steps; keep them in a JSON config file so you can A/B test the flow without redeploying your entire application.
Use Claude Code to summarize client feedback loops directly into your GitHub issues to keep development aligned with onboarding pain points.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a staging environment, invite one client to test the flow, and refine the steps based on their time-to-value metrics.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your clients and start scaling your freelance business by engineering your way out of the communication trap. The tools are ready; the architecture is waiting for your touch. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
